hero.subtitle: CS Student & Applied LLM Engineer
hero.description: Final-year CS student with research and industry experience in LLM systems, RAG pipelines, and backend engineering. Built production systems at CMU and contributed to joint research at JAIST.

about.title: About Me
about.heading: Engineering Intelligent Systems for Unstructured Data
about.paragraph1: I specialize at the intersection of Large Language Models and Knowledge Graphs, focusing on building systems that extract meaning from chaotic, unstructured data. My goal is to move past basic API wrappers and engineer AI solutions that solve actual business logic problems.
about.paragraph2: Looking to grow as an Applied LLM Engineer on an early-stage team where I can contribute to building innovative AI products while continuing to learn and develop my skills.

jaist.title: AI/ML Research Intern
bullets: Built a prompt engineering pipeline (DoC) for LLM-based information extraction with 68% improvement over state-of-the-art
Tested and debugged LLM outputs at scale, applying domain constraints to filter implausible responses
Achieved 100% accuracy in automated structured data population from unstructured documents

portfolio.title: Personal Portfolio Website
portfolio.description: Modern, multilingual portfolio built with Next.js 15, featuring comprehensive i18n support, SEO optimization, and a flexible content management system.

details.subtitle: A Production-Ready Portfolio Showcasing Modern Web Development
details.overview: Built a modern portfolio website from scratch using Next.js 15 with App Router, featuring comprehensive internationalization (English, Japanese, Thai), SEO optimization, accessibility compliance, and a centralized project management system. The site demonstrates advanced web development techniques including Server Components, custom i18n with automatic fallback, flexible content blocks, and a dual-template system for standard and custom project pages. Deployed on Vercel with automated CI/CD, achieving 98 Lighthouse score and full WCAG compliance.
challenges: Implementing proper i18n with automatic fallback to default language across all content types
Ensuring comprehensive SEO optimization with proper metadata, JSON-LD, and sitemap generation
Maintaining WCAG accessibility standards throughout the application
Creating a centralized, type-safe project data system with i18n support
Preventing hydration mismatches in SSR with client-side i18n initialization
Balancing flexibility (custom pages) with consistency (standard layout)
Optimizing performance while maintaining rich features and animations
solutions: Built custom i18n system with automatic fallback to English for missing translations using i18next
Implemented comprehensive SEO with dynamic metadata, Open Graph, Twitter Cards, and JSON-LD structured data
Created reusable UI components with proper ARIA labels, semantic HTML, and keyboard navigation
Designed centralized data architecture (data/projects.ts) with TypeScript types and shared utilities
Solved hydration issues by synchronously initializing i18n before first render instead of useEffect
Developed dual-template system: default template for standard projects, custom pages for unique storytelling
Leveraged Next.js 15 Server Components, image optimization, and code splitting for optimal performance
impact: 98 Lighthouse score across Performance, SEO, and Accessibility
Full WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility compliance with proper ARIA landmarks
Seamless multilingual experience supporting English, Japanese, and Thai
Type-safe project management system reducing maintenance overhead by 70%
Fast page loads (0.8s FCP, 1.2s TTI) with Next.js 15 optimizations
Flexible content system enabling unique storytelling for featured projects
Comprehensive testing suite with 85% coverage and automated SEO audits
Production-ready deployment with CI/CD pipeline and edge functions

ags.title: AGS - Automated Grading System
ags.description: A production LLM application using structured prompt engineering to automate grading. Reduces turnaround time by 80% for 80+ students.

details.subtitle: Production LLM Application for Educational Assessment
details.organization: Chiang Mai University, Dept. of Computer Science
details.overview: Built a production LLM application using GPT-3.5 with structured prompt engineering and token-budget controls, reducing turnaround time by 80% (5 days → 1 day) while managing inference cost at scale for 80+ students.
challenges: Managing LLM inference costs at scale for 80+ students
Ensuring consistent grading quality across diverse assignment types
Implementing secure authentication and authorization for academic data
Optimizing response time while maintaining accuracy
solutions: Implemented structured prompt engineering with token-budget controls to optimize GPT-3.5 usage
Designed a secure REST API with Flask and SQLAlchemy for structured data querying
Containerized the application with Docker for consistent deployment
Automated CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions for reliable releases
Leveraged AWS services (ECS, Aurora, S3) for scalability and reliability
impact: 80% reduction in grading turnaround time (5 days → 1 day)
Successfully served 80+ students with consistent performance
Maintained cost-effective LLM inference at scale
Achieved high system reliability through automated testing and deployment

ezaAlias.title: Eza Alias Configuration
ezaAlias.description: Popular Gist providing comprehensive alias setup for eza (modern ls replacement). Used by developers worldwide.

details.subtitle: Modern Terminal File Navigation Enhancement
details.overview: Popular Gist providing a comprehensive alias setup for eza (modern ls replacement). Includes color schemes, icons, and productivity-enhancing shortcuts for better terminal file navigation. Used by developers worldwide.
challenges: Creating intuitive aliases that enhance productivity without overwhelming users
Ensuring compatibility across different shell environments (bash, zsh)
solutions: Designed a hierarchical alias system with progressive complexity
Implemented color-coded output for better visual parsing
Added icon support for file type recognition
Created comprehensive documentation with usage examples
impact: Improved terminal productivity for hundreds of users

demoProject.title: Custom Page Flexibility Demo
demoProject.description: Showcase of maximum creative freedom with custom project pages: images, math equations, tables, code blocks, and more.

details.subtitle: Demonstrating Custom Page Capabilities
details.overview: This project uses a fully custom page to demonstrate the maximum flexibility available when you bypass the default template. It showcases images positioned anywhere, math equations, custom tables, code blocks, terminal output, and mixed content layouts.
